data.title: Press Metal Likely to Post First MYR2 Billion Core Profit in 2025 — Market Talk
data.description: Press Metal Aluminium could post core profit above MYR2 billion for the first time in 2025, driven by higher aluminium prices and stable alumina costs, RHB Investment Bank analyst Iftaar Hakim Rusli says. 4Q core profit may rise sequentially and on year to MYR650 million-MYR730 million, lifting full-year earnings to MYR2.2 billion-MYR2.3 billion, he reckons. A stronger ringgit poses some risk, as over 90% of its products are exported, he says. A 2% appreciation in the currency could trim earnings by about 5%, assuming no hedging, though this may be offset by firmer aluminium prices. Tight global supply should keep prices and premiums supported, he adds. RHB maintains a buy rating on Press Metal and keeps its target price at MYR8.50. Shares are 1.1% higher at MYR7.65. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Torrent Power to acquire Nabha Power for $760 million
data.description:
India's Torrent Power Ltd said on Monday it had entered into a deal with a unit of Larsen & Toubro to acquire Nabha Power Ltd for an enterprise value of 68.89 billion rupees ($759.48 million).

($1 = 90.7070 Indian rupees)

data.title: Oil producer Maurel & Prom welcomes easing of US sanctions in Venezuela
data.description:
French oil producer Maurel & Prom on Monday welcomed measures by the United States government to allow some companies to resume oil and gas operations in Venezuela, and said it was hoping to obtain a similar authorisation as soon as possible.

The U.S. issued two general licenses on Friday giving major energy companies the scope to operate in Venezuela, which is an OPEC member state, thereby paving the way for investments in the most significant relaxation of sanctions on Venezuela since U.S. forces captured and removed President Nicolas Maduro last month.

Maurel & Prom said in a statement this was a "constructive step."

"A stable and predictable environment will contribute to unlocking value for all stakeholders," it added.

The company said its activities on the Urdaneta Oeste field, in which its unit M&P Iberoamerica holds a 40% working interest, are running satisfactorily, and that it is ready for the next phase of field redevelopment once an authorisation is issued.

data.title: Tunisia GDP Annual Growth Advances to 2.7% in Q4
data.description:
Tunisia’s GDP grew 2.7% year-on-year in the fourth quarter of 2025, accelerating from a 2.4% increase in the previous three-month period.

Growth strengthened in agriculture, forestry, and fishing (12.3% vs 11.9% in Q3), construction (4.1% vs 2.1%), manufacture of other non-metallic products (4.2% vs 1.7%), production and distribution of gas (10.6% vs 5.8%), trade and repair services (2.1% vs 1.5%), accommodation and catering (7.2% vs 7.1%), and the information and communication sector (3.7% vs 2.7%).

Meanwhile, output growth slowed in the mining sector (12.8% vs 31.7%), chemical industries (1.3% vs 3.4%), and transport and storage (0.2% vs 0.3%).

On a quarterly basis, GDP advanced 1.0%, after showing no growth in the previous quarter.

For the full year 2025, Tunisia's economy expanded 2.5%.

data.title: Accumulate Praj Industries; target of Rs 340: Prabhudas Lilladher
data.description:
Prabhudas Lilladher's research report on Praj Industries

The company reported a weak quarter, with revenue rising marginally by 1.4% YoY and EBITDA margin contracting by 124bps YoY to 5.6%. Praj’s GenX facility secured a breakthrough order for CCUS skids, marking the start of production at the facility, albeit outside the originally envisaged ETCA pipeline, while management continues to expect FY27 to be the breakeven year for GenX with a targeted order intake of ~Rs5.0bn. Domestically, the slowdown in greenfield ethanol projects continues to weigh on BioEnergy order inflows, even as supportive policy developments in export markets aid international growth, with recent tariff reductions and the India–EU FTA expected to further enhance competitiveness overseas. Meanwhile, Praj’s diversification across CBG, Biobitumen, biopolymers, SAF and data centers is gradually gaining traction.

Outlook

The stock is trading at a P/E of 27.6x/20.6x on FY27/28E. We cut our FY27E EPS estimates by -8.0%, factoring in lower domestic demand and execution delays in BioEnergy. We upgrade our rating from ‘Hold’ to ‘Accumulate’ due to recent sharp correction in the stock price valuing the stock at a PE of 26x Sep’27E (same as earlier) arriving at a revised TP of Rs340 (Rs353 earlier).

For all recommendations report, click here

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts/broking houses/rating agencies on moneycontrol.com are their own, and not that of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: PeptiDream: Revenue fell sharply and losses widened, but a strong rebound is forecast for FY2026
data.description:
Revenue dropped 60% YoY to ¥18.5B with a net loss of ¥3.7B, driven by a sharp decline in drug discovery segment revenue, while radiopharmaceuticals grew modestly. FY2026 guidance anticipates a strong rebound to ¥32B revenue and ¥4.6B operating profit, with continued R&D investment and facility expansion.

Original document: Peptidream Inc. [4587] Annual Report — Feb. 16 2026

Disclaimer
